title: Roasted Norwegian Cabbage Casserole
description: A hearty and comforting Norwegian dish featuring roasted cabbage leaves in a rich soffritto-base, perfect for a cold winter's night. This nut-free friendly casserole is sure to become a family favorite.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Remove the tough outer leaves from the cabbage and cut it into large wedges.
In a large bowl, toss the cabbage wedges with olive oil, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the cabbage out in a single layer on a large baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes.
While the cabbage is roasting, heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions, carrots, and celery to the skillet and cook until they are softened and lightly browned.
Add the beef broth to the skillet and bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
In a large casserole dish, create a layer of the soffritto mixture, followed by a layer of roasted cabbage.
Repeat the layers until all of the ingredients have been used, ending with a layer of soffritto on top.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 25 minutes, then remove the foil and bake for an additional 10 minutes.
